items.title: US intel reportedly says Iran's military is FAR from decimated as Israel begins to worry about Trump's deal-making
items.desc: US intelligence officials have contradicted Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth, claiming that Iran's missile capabilities have not been decimated, while Israeli leaders worry about a potential deal.

items.title: Supreme Court condition for releasing haredi education budget
items.desc: The Supreme Court intends to order the release of 98 million shekels frozen for Haredi educational institutions, provided the funds are transferred only to institutions that teach fundamental subjects.

items.title: Florida congressional race erupts into antisemitism controversy
items.desc: Remarks by GOP Rep. Randy Fine and congressional candidate Dan Bilzerian trigger backlash from Armenian-American groups amid widening dispute on Israel, antisemitism, and alliances.

items.title: Thai growth likely to top 3% over next 1-2 years on new investments, minister says
items.desc: BANGKOK, May 13 : Thailand's finance minister said on Wednesday that he expects growth to top 3 per cent over the next one to two years, boosted by new investments, with the central bank also stating it was in no rush to raise interest rates. • Finance Minist…

items.title: Enough is enough. UK must do much more to tackle anti-semitism
items.desc: The recent terror attack in Golders Green must be a turning point, Sir Martin Sorrell writes in this personal opinion piece.

items.title: Thai Rate Panel Warns Against Broad Economic Stimulus as War Unfolds
items.desc: Thailand’s central bank cautioned against broad consumption stimulus, saying such a move could constrain fiscal flexibility as the economic fallout from the Middle East conflict continues to unfold.

items.title: Dubai fragrance brand Kayali pairs perfume purchases with three months of Calm
items.desc: Kayali's May campaign offers a free Calm subscription with every order, alongside a USD 100K donation to support displaced Palestinian women.

items.title: West Asia crisis yet to impact inflation, but fuel prices pass-through to shape outlook: SBI report
items.desc: The ongoing tensions in West Asia have not yet significantly impacted India's retail inflation, but the future trajectory of inflation will depend on fuel price pass-through and progress in summer crop coverage, according to a report by State Bank of India.

items.title: CFTC backs prediction market Kalshi in appeals court fight against Ohio
items.desc: Kalshi has been backed up by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission in its court fight with Ohio, with the agency asking an appeals court to affirm its jurisdiction over prediction markets.

items.title: Europe’s Oil, Gas Lobbies Urge Flexibility on Storage Targets
items.desc: European Union energy lobby groups called for more flexibility in reaching the bloc’s natural gas storage targets, to avoid market pressure during the summer refilling season.

items.title: When God Asks Powerful Probing Questions - The Crosswalk Devotional - May 13
items.desc: Unprocessed wounds, fear, and catastrophic thinking can lead to self-sabotaging decisions. When experiencing pain and injustice, often, all we want is relief. But what happens when our desire for relief leads us to greater danger?
